title: Finding NHR mutants with higher sensitivity to nematocide drugs
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8lp85","text":"This protocol explores the sensitivity of C. elegans Nuclear Hormone Receptor (NHR) mutants to known nematocidial drugs using pixel variation as the data output for paralysed/killed worms. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":42,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Making adult worm population Pick 20 worms onto a OP50-seeded 100 mm plate with NGM and incubate the worms at 25°C for ~24h.
Making adult worm population Remove the adults from the plate by picking. Incubate plates at 20°C until Day -1.
Wash adult worms off the 100 mm plate and transfer to a 15 mL Falcon tube. Wash once in M9.
Prepare the drug-plate in a deep-well 96-well plate.
Drop the adult worms onto a OP50-seeded 100 mm plates with NGM and incubate at 25°C for 4h.
Wash adults off the plates. Loosen the eggs from the plate using a plastic sterile loop and M9 and transfer to a 15 mL falcon tube. Wash twice in M9 and transfer the eggs to the 60 mm plate with M9.
Leave the 60 mm plate with eggs and M9 in the 20°C incubator O/N to synchronise.
Making PFA-killed OP50 Make an OP50 O/N culture by pipetting 20 µL of maintenance OP50 culture (OD = 1.0) into 1 L LB broth. Leave shaking at 200 rpm and 37°C O/N.

Making PFA-killed OP50 Make PFA-treated OP50 (OD = 2.0) out of the OP50 O/N culture using the protocol linked below. Final resuspension of the pellets should be done with S-medium. PFA treatment of OP50 by Bonnie Evans

Adding solvent negative controls to drug-plate: In row E , add 100 µL of sterile water to each of the wells. In row F , add 3 µL of DMSO to each of the wells. Next, add 97 µL of sterile water and mix thouroughly by pipetting up and down ~30 times.

Make Stock 1 concentrations of Aldicarb, Levamisole and Chlorpromazine. Stock 1 - Aldicarb (24 mM): Weigh 10 mg in a 5 mL Eppendorf tube. Add 2190 µL of sterile water. Vortex extensively to dissolve. Stock 1 - Levamisole (50 mM): Weigh 10 mg in a 2 mL Eppendorf tube. Add 831 µL of sterile water. Vortex extensively to dissolve. Stock 1 - Chlorpromazine (20 mM): Weigh 10 mg in a 2 mL Eppendorf tube. Add 831 µL of sterile water. Vortex extensively to dissolve.

Make 1 mL Stock 2 concentrations of Aldicarb, Levamisole and Chlorpromazine in 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes. Stock 2 - Aldicarb (4.8 mM) Add 200 µL of Stock 1 Aldicarb (24 mM) to 800 µL sterile water. Vortex. Stock 2 - Levamisole (1.2 mM) Add 24 µL of Stock 1 Levamisole (50 mM) to 976 µL sterile water. Vortex. Stock 2 - Chlorpromazine (1.2 mM) Add 60 µL of Stock 1 Chlorpromazine (20 mM) to 940 µL sterile water. Vortex.
Store Stock 1 and Stock 2 vials in -20°C.

Make Stock 1 concentrations of Ivermectin (always made fresh on the day of the experiment). Stock 1 - Ivermectin (12 mM): Weigh 10 mg in a 2 mL Eppendorf tube. Add 952 µL of DMSO. Vortex extensively to dissolve.

Make the working concentrations for Ivermectin in individual 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes for each 12 wells with different concentrations according to the table below (colour-coded according to the concentrations in the wells and which concentration to use to make a new, lower concentration). Stock 2 Ivermectin (96 µM): Add 4 µL of Stock 1 Ivermectin (12 mM) to 496 µL DMSO. Vortex.

Adding Aldicarb to drug-plate: In row A , add the volumes of sterile water according to the table below first (colour-coded according to the concentrations in the wells and which concentration to use to make a new, lower concentration). Next, add Aldicarb according to the table below and mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down ~30 times.

Adding Levamisole to drug-plate: In row B , add the volumes of sterile water according to the table below first (colour-coded according to the concentrations in the wells and which concentration to use to make a new, lower concentration). Next, add Levamisole according to the table below and mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down ~30 times.

Adding Chlorpromazine to drug-plate: In row D , add the volumes of sterile water according to the table below first (colour-coded according to the concentrations in the wells and which concentration to use to make a new, lower concentration). Next, add Chlorpromazine according to the table below and mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down ~30 times.

Adding Ivermectin to drug-plate: In row C , add 3 µL of the Ivermectin concentrations into their respective wells according to the table above. Add 97 µL of sterile water to each of the wells in row C. Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down ~30 times.
Using the ViaFlow, add 10 µL of the drugs onto 7 flat and round Nunclon 96-well plates.
Using the ViaFill with the small tubing, prime the tubing. Then dispense 30 µL of the PFA-killed OP50 (OD=2.0) to all plates. Wash the tubing with MilliQ water.
Spin down the now synchronised L1s at Program 1 and wash with fresh M9 once.
Adjust the volume of M9 to acquire ~20 worms per 20 µL by pipetting 20 µL onto a glass slide or spare plate lid with a 200 µL pipette tip.

Add a magnetic stirrer bar to the glass bottle with the worms and place the bottle on the magnetic stirrer. Change the volume of the ViaFill programme to 20 µL. Prime the small tubing with the worms. As soon as liquid is clearly coming out of all 8 tubes, start dispensing worms into 96-well plates.
Check all wells to ensure worms were dispensed in all wells.
Incubate the plates at 20°C for 72h (Day 3)

After 72h since adding the worms to the plates, record the plates 4 wells at a time according to the specifications below using the Basler camera attached to a light microscope and the Basler software 'pylon Viewer' (6.2.4.9387). Folder structure: Save images in the correct folder: Ensure you have 400 GB free space on your computer. Copy the filepath to the folder on your local computer (e.g. /Users/jb3623/Desktop/basler/video/Exp2_N2/250226_72h/plate_2/A1_B2) For each new recording, change the subdirectory name from e.g. 'A1_B2' to 'A3_B4'. Plate positioning: It is essential that the plate is oriented according to the example below (starting with A1_B2). Click on the 'scope' symbol indicated by the red arrow to activate the grid. Horizontal line crosses the sections where the top and bottom wells meet on both the left and the right side. Vetical line crosses the two wells on the right hand side just before the dark rings. Recording Settings: Output format: Tiff Recording buffer size: 10 frame(s) Record a frame every: 5 Frame(s) Stop recording after: 10 seconds
Plate layout of the final concentrations in 96 well plates to achive through the below steps (not drug-plate):
Plate layout of the final concentrations in 96 well plates to achive through the below steps (not drug-plate):

Adding Ivermectin to drug plate: If drug plates are made for freezing at -20°C, do not add Ivermectin to drug plates meant for freezing. Final DMSO concentration: 0.5% Prepare the four concentrations in four 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes according to the table below, starting from Stock 1 Ivermectin (colour coding is according to which concentration to use to make the new dilution). Prepare four new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes and add 27 µL of each of the concentrations in respective tubes. Next, add 873 µL S. H2O to the tubes and vortex. In row C , add 290 µL of the Ivermectin concentrations into their respective wells according to the table below.

Adding Aldicarb to drug plate: Prepare four 2 mL Eppendorf tubes with the concentrations as listed in the table below (colour coding is according to which concentration to use to make the new dilution). In row A , add 280 µL of each of the Aldicarb concentrations to the drug plate according to the 96-well plate layout.

If drug plates are made for freezing at -20°C, do not add controls to drug plates meant for freezing. Add Sterile H 2 O control to drugplate In row E , add 290 µL of sterile H 2 O to all 12 wells. Make DMSO control and add to drugplate Final DMSO concentration: 0.5% In three Eppendorf tubes, mix 27 µL of DMSO with 873 µL S.H2O by vortexing. in row F , add 290 µL of diluted DMSO to all 12 wells.

Adding Levamisole to drug plate: Prepare four 2 mL Eppendorf tubes with the concentrations as listed in the table below, starting from Stock 2 Levamisole (colour coding is according to which concentration to use to make the new dilution). In row B , add 280 µL of each of the Levamisole concentrations to the drug plate according to the 96-well plate layout.

Adding Chlorpromazine to drug plate: Prepare four 2 mL Eppendorf tubes with the concentrations as listed in the table below, starting from Stock 2 Chlorpromazine (colour coding is according to which concentration to use to make the new dilution). In row D , add 280 µL of each of the Chlorpromazine concentrations to the drug plate according to the 96-well plate layout.

Estimate the number of eggs on the plates in the morning. If there is a high number of eggs on the plate of adult worms, proceed from Step 11 If there is not enough number of eggs on the plate of adult worms, proceed from Step 9
Using the ViaFlow, add 10 µL of the drugs into the flat and round Nunclon 96-well plates.
Remove the two individual tubes in a small ViaFill casette that would dispense liquid into the bottom two rows in a 96 well plate (rows G & H).

Per strain: Prime the small tubing with the worms. As soon as liquid is clearly coming out of all 6 tubes, start dispensing worms into 96-well plates. Remove the tubes from the falcon tube and start priming to empty the tubes of the worms. Wash the tubes with MilliQ water by a full 5 seconds priming. Remove the tubes from the MilliQ water and prime until the tubes are empty of liquid.
Check the plates for worms by looking at the wells in the plate in a 'V' to ensure worms were dispensed in all wells.
Incubate the plates at 20°C for 72h (Day 3)
Change the volume of the ViaFill programme to 20 µL.
Prime ViaFill tubes with the PFA-killed OP50 (OD=2.0). Dispense 30 µL to all plates. Wash the tubing with MilliQ water.

Transfer the now synchronised L1s to individual 15 mL falcon tubes. Adjust the volume of M9 in the 15 mL falcon tubes to acquire ~20 worms per 20 µL by pipetting 20 µL onto a glass slide or spare plate lid with a 200 µL pipette tip.

After 72h post adding worms to plates, record the plates with four wells per video according to the specifications below using the Basler camera attached to a light microscope and the Basler software 'pylon Viewer' (6.2.4.9387). Folder structure: Save images in the correct folder: Ensure you have 400 GB free space on your computer. Copy the filepath to the folder on your local computer (e.g. /Users/jb3623/Desktop/basler/video/Exp2_N2/250226_72h/plate_2/A1_B2) For each new recording, change the subdirectory name from e.g. 'A1_B2' to 'A3_B4' according to which wells are being recorded. Plate positioning: It is essential that the plate is oriented according to the example below (starting with A1_B2). Click on the 'scope' symbol indicated by the red arrow to activate the grid. Horizontal line crosses the sections where the top and bottom wells meet on both the left and the right side. Vetical line crosses the two wells on the right hand side just before the dark rings. Recording Settings: Output format: Tiff Recording buffer size: 10 frame(s) Record a frame every: 5 Frame(s) Stop recording after: 10 seconds
creator: John Bergqvist
